title: Achoriphragma lancifolium
intro: Achoriphragma lancifolium, also known as Akarkara, is a plant species belonging to the family Valerianaceae, commonly found in the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and reduce inflammation, making it beneficial for various health concerns. The bioactive constituents of Achoriphragma lancifolium include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Akarkara is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, powders, and tablets, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Achoriphragma lancifolium belongs to the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The genus Achoriphragma itself is a small group of plants, and Achoriphragma lancifolium is one of the species within it. More specifically, Achoriphragma lancifolium is a species of flowering plant native to tropical Asia. The species is further classified as an evergreen shrub or small tree. Achoriphragma lancifolium's classification is not as well-studied as some other plant species, but it is generally accepted to be within the Apocynaceae family.
